-{"id":"bd-fpvkd","title":"add mounted-path ext4 renameat2 flag-rejection coverage for NOREPLACE and EXCHANGE","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse::rename currently rejects any nonzero rename flags with EINVAL, but fuse_e2e only covers plain rename success paths. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that invoke renameat2 with RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE (or another representative nonzero flag set) via libc/ctypes, assert exact EINVAL, and verify source/destination names and contents remain unchanged. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes the public rename flag-rejection contract.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:45:29.512905710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:45:29.512905710Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-g1dum","title":"Freeze exact ThrottleInjector debug contract","description":"Replace substring-only assertions in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs::throttle_debug_format with an exact golden contract for the custom ThrottleInjector debug output, then verify via rch targeted tests plus fmt/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:28:26.528327973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:34:01.297133332Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:34:01.294662366Z","close_reason":"Froze exact ThrottleInjector debug output contract; verified via rch targeted test plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-block","golden-artifacts","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-g1ue","title":"Harden ffs-dir test coverage with property-based and boundary tests","description":"## What
Expand ffs-dir from 49 tests to 70+ with property-based tests (proptest) and additional
boundary/corruption tests.

## Why
ffs-dir has the lowest test count (49) of any non-trivial crate. Directory operations are
safety-critical: a bug in add_entry/remove_entry can corrupt the entire directory structure.
The crate has proptest as a dev-dependency but no property-based tests yet.

## Current Coverage (49 tests)
- init_dir_block: 5 tests (basic, tail, sizes, inode numbers)
- add_entry: 10 tests (split, reuse, enospc, reject, max name, types)
- remove_entry: 7 tests (coalesce, first entry, nonexistent, overlap)
- htree: 8 tests (insert, find, remove, edge cases)
- compute_dx_hash: 3 tests (deterministic, seeds, names)
- Multi-operation: 3 tests (fill, cycle, alternating)
- Corruption detection: 3 tests (rec_len zero, misaligned, exceeds block)

## Gaps to Fill
1. Property-based tests (proptest):
   - proptest_add_remove_roundtrip: random names (1..255 bytes), random inodes,
     add then remove → block parseable, entry gone
   - proptest_add_never_exceeds_block: random sequence of adds → never writes past limit
   - proptest_init_parse_roundtrip: random self_ino, parent_ino, reserved_tail →
     init_dir_block → parse_dir_block → exactly . and .. with correct inodes
   - proptest_htree_insert_always_sorted: random hash sequence → entries always sorted

2. Boundary tests:
   - add_entry with exactly 0 bytes of slack (entry fits perfectly, no split needed)
   - remove_entry of last entry in block (rec_len extends to block end)
   - init_dir_block with reserved_tail = block_size - 24 (minimum viable)
   - add_entry with name at every alignment boundary (1,2,3,4,5,8,12,16 bytes)

3. Corruption resilience:
   - add_entry with rec_len pointing past block boundary
   - remove_entry with circular rec_len chain (entry points back to itself)
   - add_entry to block of all zeros (no valid first entry)

4. Stress tests:
   - Fill 4096-byte block completely, verify parse_dir_block recovers all entries
   - Add 100 entries, remove 50, add 50 more, verify final state

## Implementation Plan
Add to the existing #[cfg(test)] module in crates/ffs-dir/src/lib.rs.
proptest is already a dev-dependency of ffs-dir.

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p ffs-dir --lib → all pass, count >= 70
- proptest cases: 128 per property test
- No flakes in 3 consecutive runs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 4+ property-based tests added
- [ ] 5+ boundary tests added
- [ ] 3+ corruption resilience tests added
- [ ] 2+ stress tests added
- [ ] Total test count >= 70
- [ ] Zero test flakes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:18:00.522669212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:36.913247920Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:36.912968691Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -1057,7 +1057,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-spik","title":"Implement 6 parity features: btrfs decompression, ext4 inline/indirect/bigalloc/ZERO_RANGE, btrfs subvol","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T22:57:08.050698719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:57:23.369345832Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:57:23.368707223Z","close_reason":"Full parity session: implemented 6 new filesystem features to close gaps between FrankenFS and real ext4/btrfs images:

1. btrfs transparent decompression (ZLIB + ZSTD) — compressed extents now readable
2. ext4 inline data read — EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL files now readable from inode body + xattr
3. ext4 indirect block addressing — pre-extent inodes with direct/indirect/double/triple pointers
4. ext4 bigalloc infrastructure — cluster_ratio field in FsGeometry
5. ext4 FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE — new fallocate mode for zeroing allocated ranges
6. btrfs subvolume mount — configurable subvol_objectid in BtrfsContext

Also fixed during review: compressed extent size overflow, decompressed length validation, inline data xattr parsing (use parse_ibody_xattrs instead of naive offset skip).

Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md: 80/80 tracked features (was 75/75).
473 ffs-core tests pass. All leaf crates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-sqc5g","title":"add mounted-path ext4 hard-link refusal coverage for occupied destination EEXIST and non-directory parent ENOTDIR","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FUSE E2E proves hard-link success plus some refusal paths, but it does not freeze the operator-visible errors for linking onto an occupied destination or into a non-directory parent even though ffs-core already enforces EEXIST and ENOTDIR. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes that assert hard_link over an existing destination returns EEXIST and hard_link into a regular-file parent returns ENOTDIR, with unchanged nlink and directory contents. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes ext4 hard-link refusal behavior for occupied-destination and non-directory-parent cases.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:09:23.154185107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:11:49.540633974Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sqtrq","title":"stress fuse inode lock ordering under concurrent requests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:34:13.423718762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T01:22:03.963392029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T01:22:03.960982426Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-sv1b2","title":"btrfs core: resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount selections","description":"Survey finding: `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `README.md` both claim btrfs subvolume mount support, but `OpenFs::from_device` still hardcodes `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` and the operator-facing CLI rejects `--subvol/--snapshot`. This bead owns the core selection logic in `ffs-core`.

Scope:
- Extend `OpenOptions` with an explicit btrfs mount selection contract (default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and optionally numeric objectid if that simplifies diagnostics).
- Walk the root tree and resolve the requested selection to the mounted `subvol_objectid` plus `subvol_root_dirid`.
- Return deterministic `FfsError` variants for missing selections, ambiguous names, and selection/type mismatches.
- Keep default-root behavior unchanged when no selection is requested.

Acceptance:
- `OpenFs::open_with_options` can mount a non-default subvolume/snapshot without post-open mutation hacks.
- Synthetic and/or kernel-reference tests prove correct resolution for default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and missing target cases.
- Structured diagnostics include the requested selector and resolved objectid/root dirid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:23:30.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:23:30.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mount","parity"]}
+{"id":"bd-sv1b2","title":"btrfs core: resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount selections","description":"Survey finding: `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `README.md` both claim btrfs subvolume mount support, but `OpenFs::from_device` still hardcodes `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` and the operator-facing CLI rejects `--subvol/--snapshot`. This bead owns the core selection logic in `ffs-core`.

Scope:
- Extend `OpenOptions` with an explicit btrfs mount selection contract (default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and optionally numeric objectid if that simplifies diagnostics).
- Walk the root tree and resolve the requested selection to the mounted `subvol_objectid` plus `subvol_root_dirid`.
- Return deterministic `FfsError` variants for missing selections, ambiguous names, and selection/type mismatches.
- Keep default-root behavior unchanged when no selection is requested.

Acceptance:
- `OpenFs::open_with_options` can mount a non-default subvolume/snapshot without post-open mutation hacks.
- Synthetic and/or kernel-reference tests prove correct resolution for default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and missing target cases.
- Structured diagnostics include the requested selector and resolved objectid/root dirid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:23:30Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:23:30Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mount","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-sv2c3","title":"ffs-cli: wire btrfs --subvol and --snapshot mount flags to OpenOptions","description":"Survey finding: `ffs-cli` advertises `--subvol`/`--snapshot` on `mount`, but `validate_btrfs_mount_selection()` hard-rejects both flags even though the underlying codebase already has internal btrfs subvolume context machinery. This bead owns the operator-facing CLI plumbing.

Scope:
- Remove the unconditional rejection path in `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`.
- Thread the requested selection into `OpenOptions` and preserve all existing ext4 behavior.
- Emit structured startup/rejection logs that show requested selector, resolved selection type, and failure class.
- Replace the current `mount_rejects_subvol_flag` / `mount_rejects_snapshot_flag` tests with positive routing tests and deterministic failure diagnostics for nonexistent selectors.

Acceptance:
- `ffs mount ... --subvol <name>` and `ffs mount ... --snapshot <name>` reach `OpenFs::open_with_options` instead of bailing in argument validation.
- Missing selectors fail with clear, stable user-facing errors.
- CLI tests cover default-root, explicit subvolume, explicit snapshot, and nonexistent selection cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:14:12.819010463Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:14:12.819010463Z","close_reason":"Added CLI OpenOptions coverage plus stable missing-selector tests; workspace check/clippy/fmt and focused ffs-cli btrfs tests passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","mount"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv2c3","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-sv3d4","title":"add operator-facing coverage and parity evidence for btrfs subvolume/snapshot mount","description":"Survey finding: current conformance only proves the default btrfs root-alias path, while docs/parity wording imply user-selectable subvolume mount support. This bead closes that evidence gap after the core and CLI work land.

Scope:
- Add harness and/or FUSE E2E coverage that mounts a non-default btrfs subvolume and snapshot through the real CLI/operator path.
- Assert mounted-root `getattr`/`lookup`/`readdir`/`read` behavior is scoped to the selected tree, not the default root.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and README wording so the operator-facing support claim matches the tested surface exactly.
- Ensure test output/logs emit machine-parseable scenario identifiers for success and failure cases.

Acceptance:
- There is deterministic coverage for mounting a named subvolume and a named snapshot via the public mount path.
- README and `FEATURE_PARITY.md` no longer overclaim relative to the tested operator surface.
- Coverage artifacts make it clear whether support is internal-only or operator-visible.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","docs","e2e","mount","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv2c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-svxc3","title":"add mounted-path fallocate invalid PUNCH_HOLE-without-KEEP_SIZE rejection coverage for ext4 and btrfs","description":"Survey finding: ffs-core already rejects fallocate(PUNCH_HOLE) without KEEP_SIZE for both ext4 and btrfs, but mounted-path fuse_e2e only freezes success paths and unsupported extra-mode-bit rejection. Scope: add real FUSE probes on ext4 and btrfs mounts that invoke invalid PUNCH_HOLE-without-KEEP_SIZE, assert exact EINVAL, and verify file size and data remain unchanged. Acceptance: mounted-path fallocate coverage distinguishes invalid punch-hole mode from unsupported extra-bit rejection on both filesystems.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:11:26.592121204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:27:21Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:27:21Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -102,6 +102,9 @@ workspace-entry or parent-file drift.
 Mounted-path btrfs symlink refusal coverage now also freezes exact `EEXIST` for
 occupied destinations and exact `ENOTDIR` for non-directory parents, with no
 visible workspace-entry or parent-file drift.
+Mounted-path ext4 `renameat2` flag-rejection coverage now also freezes exact
+`EINVAL` for `RENAME_NOREPLACE` and `RENAME_EXCHANGE`, with no source,
+destination, or root-entry drift.
 Mounted-path ext4 read-only `fallocate` coverage now also freezes exact `EROFS`
 for representative preallocate and punch-hole mutation attempts, with no
 file-byte or apparent-size drift.
